lessons_learned: Adoption is binary by domain (100% or 0%), not gradual — all-or-nothing pattern across 6 domains
vocabulary-ceiling concept had 100% adoption rate (3/3) — most universal concept type
epistemic-lock had 67% adoption (2/3) — worked in domains with active experimental traditions
goodhart-cascade had 33% adoption (1/3) — only adopted where pre-existing metric concern existed (strategy)
Single-use concepts (sensor-only-trap, diagnosis-repair-gap) had 0% adoption — lacked generality

Domain activity level at injection time predicts adoption: filtering (6 resolved), strategy (3 resolved), stochastic-processes (6 resolved) all had high prior activity; security (2 resolved), governance (4 resolved), ai (3 resolved) had lower recent dispatch frequency
Adopted frontiers generated 8 lessons total (1.33 per adopted frontier), demonstrating real knowledge production not just categorization

effect_size.vs_prediction: 6 vs >=4 predicted (50% rate vs 33% predicted minimum)
effect_size.vs_falsification: 6 vs <2 falsification threshold (3x above falsification line)
